title: thebasics:thebasics-handbook-title-character-sheets
text: Character sheets are the shiny identity layer for RP: visible bios, /look details, configurable fields, headshots, and optional character slots. Server owners can enable or disable the system, choose fields, require values before RP chat, control visibility, and decide which fields appear in /look. Player commands - /charsheet or /sheet: open your editable sheet. - /charsheet <player>: view another online player's visible sheet fields. - /bio: quick view alias for character sheets. - /look or /look <player>: inspect a nearby player. Without a name, it uses the player you are targeting. - /charsheetfields: list configured sheet fields and visibility. - /setcharsheet <field> <value>: set a field from chat. Aliases include /setsheet and /setbio. - /clearcharsheet [field]: clear one field, or all editable fields when no field is given. Aliases include /clearsheet and /clearbio. /look rules /look is meant for in-character inspection. By default the target must be online, nearby, and in line of sight, but range and line-of-sight rules are configurable. Self-look opens your full editable sheet. Fields marked self-only or admin-only are hidden from other players. Nicknames and display names Nicknames belong to RP chat, but character sheets can integrate with them. When the configured nickname field exists, /nick and /nickcolor can update sheet-backed display information. The default display priority is Full Name (Nickname), then Full Name, then Nickname, then account name. Headshots When enabled, your editable sheet can accept a PNG/JPEG drop for a character portrait. Server config controls size limits and whether direct image URLs are allowed. /clearheadshot removes your portrait. Character slots If RP character slots are enabled, use /character or /characters to list slots, inspect the active slot, create/switch/delete slots, and manage per-character data. Slot limits and deletion rules are server-configurable.
